00001 /*
00002  * waitpid_syscall.c
00003  *
00004  *  Created on: June 04, 2010
00005  *      Author: Luigi Grimaudo  153740
00006  *              Piera Gueli     160119

00008 
00009 #include <types.h>
00010 #include <kern/errno.h>
00011 #include <kern/reboot.h>
00012 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00013 #include <spinlock.h>
00014 #include <wchan.h>
00015 #include <threadlist.h>
00016 #include <threadprivate.h>
00017 #include <lib.h>
00018 #include <spl.h>
00019 #include <clock.h>
00020 #include <thread.h>
00021 #include <current.h>
00022 #include <synch.h>
00023 #include <vm.h>
00024 #include <mainbus.h>
00025 #include <vfs.h>
00026 #include <device.h>
00027 #include <syscall.h>
00028 #include <kern/fcntl.h>
00029 
00030 /* Wait for the process specified by pid to exit, and return an encoded exit
00031  * status in the integer pointed to by status. If that process has exited
00032  * already, waitpid returns immediately. If that process does not exist,
00033  * waitpid fails.
00034  *
00035  */
00036 
00037 extern char pid_used[MAX_THREAD];
00038 extern struct thread* thread_table[MAX_THREAD];
00039 
00040 pid_t waitpid(pid_t pid, int *status, int options, int* retval) {
00041 
00042     int spl;
00043 
00044     /* Check if options flag is 0 */
00045     if(options != 0){
00046                 return EINVAL;
00047     }
00048 
00049     /* Check if status parameter is a valid int pointer */
00050     if (status == NULL) {
00051                 return EFAULT;
00052     }
00053 
00054     /* Check if pid is not out of bounds */
00055     if (pid > MAX_THREAD) {
00056                 return ESRCH;
00057     }
00058 
00059     /* Check if thread exist */
00060    if (pid_used[pid] == 0){
00061                 return ESRCH;
00062     }
00063     
00064     *status = thread_table[pid]->exit_code;
00065 
00066     spl = splhigh();
00067     while (pid_used[pid]==1){
00068         clocksleep(2);
00069     }
00070     splx(spl);
00071     
00072     *retval = pid;
00073 
00074     return 0;
00075 }
00076
